svg.title: Section 4.3 (N2): wasted compute on failed attempts, imperative vs SDP
svg.desc: Executor-seconds spent on attempts that ultimately failed, measured on a live EKS Spark-Connect cluster. Bare imperative wastes 521 executor-seconds because spark-submit executes over the data and only then discovers the fault. SDP wastes about 0.5 executor-seconds because its dry-run rejects a bad pipeline before any executor starts. The ratio is roughly one thousand times, finite versus about zero. At production scale a fleet authoring a thousand pipelines a week would waste about five thousand dollars a month of compute that SDP never spends.
svg.text: WASTED COMPUTE ON FAILED ATTEMPTS · MEASURED ON LIVE EKS (48-CELL SWEEP)
executor-seconds spent on attempts that ultimately failed
spark-submit executes over the data, then the fault surfaces, so the compute is already spent
the dry-run rejects the bad pipeline before any executor starts, so ≈0 is wasted
also: ~34× total compute · ~7× cost per correct pipeline
At production scale (projection from the measured mechanism):
a fleet authoring ~1,000 pipelines/week wastes ≈ $5,000/month of compute that SDP's gate never spends.
